title: 'Only one neighbour is troublesome': Rajnath's 'dot, dot, dot' warning to Pak on Pahalgam anniversary
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh issued a stern warning to Pakistan, stating that any attempt to create trouble would be met with a strong response. He highlighted India's enhanced military strength and its shift from import dependence to becoming a defence exporter. Singh's remarks come a year after the Pahalgam terror attack.

title: AI-generated MAGA influencer: Indian student behind 'hot girl' profile with millions of followers
content: An India-based medical student created and operated a popular AI-generated Instagram influencer, "Emily Hart," who amassed millions of followers with patriotic and political content targeting conservative US audiences. The virtual persona, presented as a registered nurse, was monetized through merchandise and subscription platforms, generating thousands of dollars monthly before the account was removed for fraudulent activity.

title: Pakistan’s Nawaz in trouble after positive drug test; PCB to probe
content: Pakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz is under investigation by the PCB after testing positive for recreational drugs during the 2026 T20 World Cup. The 32-year-old's participation in the tournament was modest, and this development has already led to his withdrawal from a county cricket contract. The outcome of the PCB's due process will determine his future.

title: Tim Cook gives employees three reasons he chose now to step down as Apple CEO
content: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO after 15 years, deeming it the 'textbook succession' he envisioned. He cited a thriving business, a robust product pipeline including a foldable iPhone and AI wearables, and hardware chief John Ternus's readiness as key factors. Ternus, set to take over, expressed excitement about Apple 'changing the world once again' with AI.

title: '70-80% of politicians watch ...': Pappu Yadav stokes fresh controversy after 'bedroom' remark
content: Bihar MP Pappu Yadav courted fresh controversy by claiming 70-80% of politicians watch porn, while defending his earlier sexist remarks about women in politics. The Bihar State Women's Commission has sought an explanation from Yadav for his offensive statements, which have drawn widespread criticism.

title: Middle East on edge again: Two ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
content: Tensions flared in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran's Revolutionary Guard fired on a container ship, causing damage. Another cargo vessel reported being fired upon. These incidents complicate US-Iran diplomatic efforts, despite a US ceasefire extension and Pakistan's mediation. The strategic waterway, crucial for global energy, remains under pressure amid escalating hostilities.

title: Map quest: Virginia vote leaves Trump seeing red – and blue
content: Virginia voters approved a referendum granting the Democratic-controlled legislature power to redraw congressional maps, potentially shifting the state's representation from a 6-5 Democratic edge to a 10-1 dominance. This redistricting battle, ignited by Republican efforts in Texas and countered by Democrats in California, could significantly impact the upcoming U.S. House of Representatives elections, threatening the Republican majority.

title: Meta to install tracking software on American employee's machines: Here is what the memo said
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' day 5: Akshay Kumar film eyes Rs. 100 crore domestic milestone
content: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is chasing the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it enters Day 6 of its theatrical run. With a worldwide gross of Rs. 118.12 crore, the horror-comedy has recovered strongly from its Monday dip with an 18.5% growth on Day 5. The film opened with Rs. 12.25 crore on Day 1, peaked at Rs. 23 crore on Day 3 and is now building toward a healthy first week total. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the supernatural comedy-thriller follows Arjun Acharya returning to his ancestral home to confront the demonic entity Vadhusur. Stay updated with all live Day 6 tracking, day-wise collections and the complete box office story of Bhooth Bangla.

title: Watch: IAF aircraft lands on UP’s Purvanchal Expressway to test wartime preparedness
content: The Indian Air Force conducted takeoff and landing drills on the Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur using an Airbus C295 aircraft. This exercise assessed the expressway's viability as an emergency landing field during wartime. Fighter jets also participated in a flypast, highlighting the IAF's operational readiness.

title: Mhatre to Banton: 5 players ruled out of IPL 2026 due to injuries
content: IPL 2026 is already seeing significant player withdrawals due to injuries, with Chennai Super Kings and Sunrisers Hyderabad losing two players each. Tom Banton has also been ruled out. Several other key players were sidelined even before the tournament commenced, impacting team compositions midway through.

title: Odisha school headmaster puts stinging nettle in 10 students’ trousers as punishment
content: A shocking incident unfolded at a government school in Jajpur where a headmaster allegedly inserted stinging nettles into the trousers of ten students for causing classroom disturbances. The students suffered severe itching and rashes. Following widespread condemnation and protests by parents, the headmaster has been suspended pending further action, despite offering an apology.

title: Diet Cokes ‘Can’t’? How Iran conflict is hitting supplies; retailers run out of stock
content: Diet Coke is disappearing from shelves in major Indian cities due to a shortage of aluminum cans, exacerbated by global supply disruptions. The beverage's heavy reliance on cans, coupled with surging demand for sugar-free options, makes it particularly vulnerable. Companies are exploring costlier overseas imports as domestic production struggles to meet demand.

title: ‘Tactically very good’: Why ex-India cricketer backs Kishan to lead SRH over Cummins
content: Former India coach Sanjay Bangar advocates for Ishan Kishan to retain the Sunrisers Hyderabad captaincy even after Pat Cummins' return. Bangar highlighted Kishan's tactical acumen and game control under pressure, citing his effective use of bowlers, particularly spinners. He believes continuity in leadership will benefit the franchise, especially considering potential fitness concerns for Cummins.

title: US tightens screws on high-skilled immigration: Denial rates surge across visa categories
content: US immigration routes for skilled Indian professionals are facing unprecedented uncertainty. Elite visa categories, including EB-1 and EB-2 NIW, have seen rejection rates nearly double and more than triple respectively. Even temporary visas like O-1 and L-1 are experiencing increased denials, signaling a significant tightening of pathways for top talent.

title: 2006 Malegaon blast case: Accused get court nod to challenge charges framed by NIA
content: Bombay High Court has permitted four individuals accused in the 2006 Malegaon blasts to challenge charges under the UAPA and other offenses. The court's decision allows them to contest charges including murder and attempt to murder. This development comes after the framing of 19 charges against them last year, with the NIA investigating the case.

title: Tech Mahindra Q4 results: Profit jumps 16% YoY, Rs 36/share dividend announced
content: Tech Mahindra concluded FY26 with a 13.15% rise in net profit to Rs 15,076 crore and a 7.2% revenue increase to Rs 56,815.4 crore, exceeding analyst expectations. The company is accelerating its AI-led transformation, evidenced by record deal wins, and has declared a final dividend of Rs 36 per share.

title: ‘Have to give Abhishek Sharma leeway’: SRH’s Varun Aaron explains why
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad bowling coach Varun Aaron emphasised patience with high-impact players like Abhishek Sharma, who silenced critics with an unbeaten 135 against Delhi Capitals. Aaron highlighted Abhishek's maturity and ability to adapt his game. The bowlers, led by Eshan Malinga's four wickets, successfully defended the massive total, showcasing SRH's adaptable bowling strategy.

title: Turning off transponders - How some Iran tankers carrying oil are sailing past US blockade
content: Data from Vortexa indicates that Iranian exports have persisted despite mounting pressure. At least 34 vessels linked to Iran, including tankers and gas carriers, have crossed through the Strait of Hormuz and the US blockade zone.

title: US tourist given spiked drink, raped at Karnataka homestay
content: A tourist from the United States was allegedly raped at a homestay in Kodagu district, leading to the arrest of two individuals. The victim is receiving treatment in Mysuru, and authorities are conducting a thorough investigation. The incident has drawn condemnation, with calls for swift justice and stringent action.

title: 'Why go to a part-timer?' Ashwin slams Axar’s captaincy after DC's defeat
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Delhi Capitals by 47 runs after posting 242/2, powered by Abhishek Sharma’s 135*. DC fell short at 195/9, with Eshan Malinga taking 4/32. Ravichandran Ashwin criticised Axar Patel’s captaincy, calling the decision to give part-timer Nitish Rana four overs, while frontline spinners bowled just two each, the “biggest blunder.”

title: Doctor can’t be prosecuted for choosing medically valid procedure without proof of consent tampering: Supreme Court quashes case
content: A key aspect of the case was the opinion of a Medical Board constituted pursuant to directions of the High Court. The Board examined the medical records and histopathology reports and concluded that the removed testicle was small, cystic, dysplastic, and exhibited focal fibrosis.

title: 'Thought I had understanding with Xi': Trump says ship seized had 'gift from China' for Iran
content: President Trump revealed the US seized a ship carrying "not very nice" items, calling it a "gift from China." He linked this to Iran tensions, threatening to bomb bridges and expressing no desire to extend the Iran ceasefire. Trump anticipates a "great deal" but also expects bombing, despite China denying weapon supplies to Iran.

title: Gujarat Titans' Kagiso Rabada caught smoking in hotel premises - Watch
content: Gujarat Titans bowler Kagiso Rabada is under scrutiny after being photographed smoking. While not a rule violation, the incident sparks debate given his past doping ban. Despite this, Rabada continues to perform well on the field, taking 10 wickets in six IPL 2026 matches, showcasing his ongoing value to the team.

title: Trump cuts legal immigration more than illegal crossings; students, H-1B flows hit
content: US legal immigration, particularly for students and skilled workers, has seen a steeper decline than illegal entries under the Trump administration. International students face increased visa hurdles and uncertainty, while H-1B visa issuances are pressured by higher fees and stricter rules. This trend risks impacting US innovation and competitiveness.

title: Jobless man poisons pet dog, dies by suicide in Bareilly; cousin cites debt pressure
content: A 30-year-old man and his pet dog were found dead in a locked house in Bareilly. Police suspect the man poisoned his dog before dying by suicide due to financial distress and personal heartbreak. The discovery was made after a relative alerted authorities when the man became unreachable.

title: Sooryavanshi termed next 'demigod' of cricket: Srikkanth invokes Tendulkar comparison
content: Former India captain Kris Srikkanth has urged selectors to fast-track 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the national team, comparing him to a young Sachin Tendulkar.  Rival skipper Shreyas Iyer also called him 'one for the future.' Sooryavanshi has impressed with 246 runs at a strike rate of 236.53 in IPL 2026.

title: 'It still haunts us': Pahalgam attack, one year on — scars speak between memory and recovery
content: A year after a devastating terror attack claimed 26 lives in Pahalgam, the valley grapples with lingering fear and a fractured tourism economy. India responded with Operation Sindoor, a cross-border military action, and extensive diplomatic efforts. While security has been bolstered, the region's recovery remains fragile, with businesses and residents still navigating the profound impact of the violence.

title: Ranveer to return his signing amount to settle 'Don 3' dispute with Farhan Akhtar
content: Ranveer Singh is reportedly returning his Rs 10 crore signing amount and offering a stake in his upcoming film, 'Pralay', to settle the 'Don 3' dispute with Farhan Akhtar and Ritesh Sidhwani. The fallout began after Singh's exit from the project due to script dissatisfaction. The Producers Guild of India reportedly mediated the disagreement.

title: ‘Public will punish Congress’: BJP delegation meets EC over Kharge’s ‘terrorist’ remark on PM Modi
content: The BJP lodged a complaint with the Election Commission against Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge for calling Prime Minister Narendra Modi a "terrorist." Union Minister Kiren Rijiju stated that such language is unprecedented and demanded strict action and an apology from Kharge and the Congress party.

title: ‘Ignored repeated warnings’: Iran fires on container ship in Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, causing significant damage to its bridge. While Iran claims the vessel ignored warnings, the UKMTO reported no prior alert. This incident escalates regional maritime tensions, following recent US seizures of Iranian vessels and ongoing disputes over naval blockades.

title: ‘Mere bachhe ko maar diya’: Mother confronts Meerut blue drum murder accused Muskan outside court
content: Mother of Saurabh Rajput confronted the accused in the "blue drum" murder case outside a Meerut court, expressing her grief and demanding the death penalty. Rajput, a former Merchant Navy officer, was murdered and dismembered by his wife and her alleged lover. The trial is ongoing, with 22 witness statements recorded.

title: 'Tum aaya, saath mein camera leke aaya… bhagao isko': Dhoni’s funny dig at Chahar
content: MS Dhoni and Deepak Chahar shared a humorous moment during a recent net session, with Dhoni jokingly telling a cameraman to leave so they could talk privately. This interaction highlights their strong off-field bond, often described as an "elder brother-younger brother" relationship, despite their fierce on-field rivalry.

title: TCS Nashik scandal: 9 cases, 7 arrests & now bail battle; missing woman employee still untraced
content: Seven individuals arrested in connection with sexual offense cases at TCS Nashik have sought bail. Hearings are scheduled for these pleas in the coming days. Nine cases have been registered, involving eight TCS employees accused of sexual offenses and hurting religious sentiments. Two women are among the accused, with one woman employee's whereabouts still unknown.

title: Listening to music while eating makes food taste better? Science has a surprising answer
content: Science reveals sound significantly impacts our food experience, influencing taste perception and enjoyment. Researchers found high-pitched sounds enhance sweetness, while low tones amplify bitterness. Tempo affects eating speed, and volume can alter food choices. This crossmodal connection, where the brain links sound with flavour, is already shaping restaurant experiences.

title: 'Don't need financial support, just prayers': Shapoor Zadran's fight for life in India
content: Former Afghanistan pacer Shapoor Zadran, who once stunned India’s openers Gautam Gambhir and Virender Sehwag in the 2012 T20 World Cup, is battling a rare, life-threatening disease called HLH in a New Delhi hospital. Supported by teammates like Rashid Khan, his condition remains serious, but his fighting spirit endures as he faces the toughest challenge of his life.

title: Wemby concussed on hard fall in Spurs' G2 loss
content: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama sustained a concussion on a hard fall during the second quarter of Tuesday night's Game 2 playoff loss to the Portland Trail Blazers.

title: Edgecombe makes history with 30-10 to stun C's
content: VJ Edgecombe had 30 points and 10 rebounds while playing through pain after taking a hard fall on his back early in the game, Tyrese Maxey scored 29 points and the 76ers beat the Celtics 111-97 on Tuesday night to tie their series at one game apiece.

title: Splitter on Dundon reports: 'Trying to be a pro'
content: In response to questions about reported cost-cutting measures by new Blazers owner Tom Dundon, interim coach Tiago Splitter said he's focusing on basketball.

title: Williams' collapse in 9th brings Mets' slide to 12
content: The Mets dropped their 12th straight game Tuesday, this time blowing an early 3-0 lead and allowing the Twins to score two in the ninth in a 5-3 win at Citi Field.

title: Kings coach hit by shattered glass as panel breaks
content: Kings interim coach D.J. Smith had shattered glass fall on him after a pane broke behind the Los Angeles bench Tuesday night in Game 2 against Colorado.

title: Smart's defense on KD helps Lakers to 2-0 lead
content: The Rockets had Kevin Durant back for Game 2 of their first-round matchup Tuesday. But the Lakers had Marcus Smart guard him in their 101-94 win to take a 2-0 series lead.

title: Kentucky governor blasts decision-making at UK
content: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear has publicly questioned the decision-making at the University of Kentucky, including the seven-figure gig retiring athletic director Mitch Barnhart was recently given.

title: Bruins 'played to our identity' in Game 2 victory
content: The Boston Bruins, who let a two-goal lead slip away late in Game 1 on Sunday, stormed ahead in Game 2 with four goals in the first 41 minutes, and goaltender Jeremy Swayman made 34 saves en route to a 4-2 victory over the Buffalo Sabres on Tuesday night.

title: 📸 Drake trolls Bears' QB in rollout of new album
content: The Canadian rapper posed in front of blocks of ice while wearing a Green Bay Packers jacket during his "Iceman" album rollout.

title: Tim Duncan, David Robinson ... LeBron: What Victor Wembanyama's Game 1 signals for the NBA
content: Wembanyama will always be linked to Duncan and Robinson, but after Sunday night's dominant Game 1, it was another generational star to whom he was being compared: LeBron James.

title: Fallacy or fact? Why Lakers' shooting records in Game 1 could be good news for Rockets
content: Examining how Game 1's shotmaking gap could actually be good news for Houston in its series versus L.A.

title: How the 'Lumber Yard' was built: Inside the Avalanche's goaltending tandem
content: The professional lives of Mackenzie Blackwood and Scott Wedgewood intersected twice before suiting up together for Colorado.

title: NFL Nation mock draft: Our team reporters predict landing spots in the first round
content: The Raiders fill their biggest need at No. 1, but is Fernando Mendoza the only first-round QB? Our reporters make their picks.

title: 'It's 100 or die': How throwing 100 mph went from a novelty to the norm in MLB
content: Hitting 100 mph on the radar gun is a badge of honor. Here's why the number of pitchers who have reached it has exploded.

title: 'Things that come in a dozen': Twins troll Mets after team suffers 12th straight loss
content: The Minnesota Twins made sure to poke fun at the New York Mets after handing them their 12th straight loss.

title: Meet Prospect X, who could be the 2026 NFL draft's brightest diamond in the rough
content: After months of searching, we've found the prospect we believe is the 2026 NFL draft's best-kept secret.

title: The Draft Day Predictor settles big Round 1 debates
content: Our analytics model crunches the numbers on Ty Simpson, Jeremiyah Love and how four NFL teams should approach their first-round picks.

title: How will Browns use their two first-round draft picks?
content: Cleveland's front office is seeking "difference-makers and elite-level talent" at the No. 6 and No. 24 picks.

title: What questions do Alabama, Georgia, BYU, Oregon and others face after spring ball?
content: Eleven notable teams and CFP contenders head into summer with some unresolved things that need answers.

title: NBA MVP: Who will win between Jokic, SGA and Wemby?
content: Who will win the MVP award: Nuggets' Nikola Jokic, Spurs' Victor Wembanyama or Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander?

title: Soccer's incredible shrinking shin guards could be a dangerous problem
content: Some players think that smaller shin guards -- literally the size of a small notebook -- make them faster. Others around the game think it's a major injury waiting to happen.

title: Leicester's fall from grace: From Premier League champions to relegation to League One
content: It feels like yesterday that Leicester were the Cinderellas of world soccer, winning the Premier League against all odds. On Tuesday, however, the Foxes' ignominious relegation to the third tier was confirmed.

title: Men's college basketball buzz: State of blue blood rebuilds
content: North Carolina awaits a decision from Henri Veesaar while Kansas and Kentucky vie for the No. 1 recruit.

title: Women's transfer rankings: No. 1 Audi Crooks commits to Oklahoma State
content: Audi Crooks is headed to Oklahoma St., Jordan Lee is bound for South Carolina and Kymora Johnson is staying at U.Va.

title: The biggest question facing every WNBA team in training camp
content: How will Angel Reese fit the Dream? Will the Wings return to the playoffs? Can Caitlin Clark remain healthy?

title: WrestleMania 42 Night 2 takeaways: Will Reigns make this title run alone?
content: Roman Reigns is champion once again, but what should fans expect in the latest title reign of the OTC?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United in for Juventus midfielder
content: Manchester United are interested in signing Juventus midfielder Teun Koopmeiners. Join us for the latest transfer news and rumors from around the globe.

title: F1's rule tweaks: What's changed after driver criticism, and will it make a difference?
content: Formula 1 has delivered a set of rules tweaks to its controversial 2026 regulations -- but what do they mean and will they appease drivers ahead of the Miami GP? ESPN's Laurence Edmondson explains.

title: Prospect watch: Is Cleveland ready for a new catcher?
content: All the prospects fantasy managers should be keeping an eye on as the season continues to move through its first month.

title: Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon lead way-too-early 2026 rankings
content: It's never too early to plan for next season with the fantasy hockey way-too-early rankings for 2026-27.

content: Scouting reports and fantasy-relevant player picks from each team in the WNBA, including the two new expansions.

content: Everything you need to know before your fantasy women's basketball draft, including rankings, tips and news.

title: How Iran's "Mosquito Fleet" Puts Global Oil Flow At Risk
content: These boats, operated by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, are cheap, quick, and difficult to track.

title: Oil Spills From US-Israeli War On Iran Are So Large They're Visible From Space
content: The spilt oil has the potential to affect the lives and livelihoods of people along the Gulf coastlines, as well as the area’s abundant marine life.

title: How India's Defence Spending Surged, Strategy Shifted Post Op Sindoor
content: After the Pahalgam attack, the government put the Indus Water Treaty in abeyance. It also announced that an act of terror will be an "act of war".

title: The Mojtaba Khamenei Factor In US-Iran Peace Talks Delay
content: The White House believes that with Khamenei absent from the active decision-making process, there were fractures within the current Iranian leadership.

title: Fearing "Curse Of 35", Young Chinese Start "One-Person Companies" Using AI
content: More young people in China are subscribing to the model of "one-person companies", as cities pledge millions of dollars in funding and rent subsidies.

title: BJP Seeks Campaign Ban On M Kharge Over Remark Against PM Modi
content: The BJP has urged the poll body to take immediate and strict action against Mallikarjun Kharge over his controversial remarks, calling it a serious breach of election norms and submitted a detailed complaint.

title: 5 Crore Voters, 3 Lakh Officials: Tamil Nadu Gears Up For Key Polls
content: In a major crackdown on electoral malpractice, officials reported record seizures - Rs 1,262 crore intercepted and Rs 543 crore worth confiscated.

title: Opinion: Opinion | How A War Destroyed America's Strongest Financial Weapon, Built Over 50 Years
content: America's own Secretary of State has publicly conceded that its most powerful financial weapon may be obsolete within five years.

title: Kerala Factory Blast: Probe Team Formed, Rs 14-Lakh Aid Announced
content: Thirteen people died in Kerala's Mundathikode fireworks explosion with 15 injured undergoing treatment.

title: Donald Trump Weighs Shipping Waiver Renewal To Ease Oil, Gas Prices
content: Global energy prices have risen substantially in the past two months, with Brent crude, the international benchmark, climbing nearly 36 per cent.

title: Laps Of Icy Roads In China Show Sodium Batteries Making An EV Breakthrough
content: The tests were a showcase for one of the world's first mass-production passenger EVs fitted with the alternative technology.

title: Vladimir Putin's Oil Windfall Won't Revive Russia's Slowing Wartime Economy
content: The surging cost of oild ue to US war with Iran is boosting Russia's export income to the highest since the early weeks of Russia-Ukraine conflict.

title: DMK's PTR Banks on Legacy, Track Record To Secure Hat-Trick On Home Turf
content: PTR is an investment banker-turned-politician and represents the fourth generation of his family’s long political legacy.

title: Webinar on impact of screen time on mental health to be held on April 25
content: The webinar will focus on the impact of screen time on mental health, its effects on behaviour and well-being, risks such as sleep disruption and anxiety, and the importance of mindful usage and awareness in maintaining a healthy balance

title: Ex-Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Nadendla Bhaskara Rao dies at 90
content: Mr. Bhaskara Rao, who served as Chief Minister for 31 days in 1984 after a political revolt against N.T. Rama Rao, died at a private hospital in Hyderabad on Wednesday; his last rites will be performed on April 24

title: Initiative launched for waste management along Karnataka’s coastline
content: Spanning three years, the initiative aims to address the plastic waste challenge along Karnataka’s coastline, where plastic accounts for 60% of the coastal litter

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: West Bengal's identity is not associated with Kaaba, but with Maa Kalibari, says Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath
content: Addressing a rally in Kolkata's Jorasanko Assembly area, the Uttar Pradesh CM said, "The lawlessness that we're witnessing here is similar to the lawlessness that existed in Uttar Pradesh before 2017. Today, there is no unrest in U.P.”

title: Of MGR, Sivaji Ganesan, and Tamil Nadu’s star-studded politics
content: Actors with huge fan followings, such as Sivaji Ganesan, Kamal Haasan, and Vijayakant, as well as popular stars like K. Bhagyaraj, Karthik, and Ramarajan, have tried their luck in elections with limited success

title: West Bengal’s defections: 2021 and beyond
content: After the 2021 Assembly elections, over a dozen legislators who had switched from TMC to BJP returned to the ruling party, eroding the saffron party’s strength

title: IRS officer's daughter found murdered in southeast Delhi's Amar Colony
content: According to police, the victim was discovered by her family members inside the house under suspicious circumstances, following which the police were alerted.

title: Kerala fireworks accident: 9 victims identified; fireworks-free Thrissur Pooram under consideration
content: Five bodies handed over to relatives; two injured on ventilator as authorities weigh key decisions amid tragedy

title: Thrissur fireworks accident: Kerala Cabinet orders judicial inquiry, ₹14-lakh aid to families of deceased
content: Cabinet directs State Disaster Management Authority to declare blast at Mundathikode as a State-specific disaster; Justice C.N. Ramachandran Nair, a single-member judicial commission, in charge of inquiry

title: Actor Nusrat appears before ED in Kolkata in connection with housing project fraud probe
content: "She has been summoned in connection with an ongoing investigation into a housing fraud case.” an ED official said

title: Kerala govt declares Thrissur fireworks accident State-specific disaster
content: Revenue Minister Rajan says decision was taken in a special online Cabinet meeting chaired by Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan

title: Kerala fireworks accident: Special team to probe cause of explosion in Thrissur that killed 13
content: State Police Chief to hold high-level meeting with police, Revenue, Fire and Rescue Services departments, and Petroleum and Explosives Safety Organisation officials in Thrissur after visiting accident site

title: Kerala fireworks accident: Decision on Thrissur Pooram after Cabinet meet, says Devaswom Minister
content: Vasavan says meeting involving Paramekkavu and Thiruvambady Devaswoms, which organise the festival, along with the district administration and officials concerned, will be held on Thursday, as permitted by Kerala High Court

title: Delhi High Court judge recuses from hearing contempt PIL against Kejriwal over circulation of court videos
content: Justice Karia, a former partner at a leading law firm, represented Meta in several cases before his elevation as a judge

title: Falling tile at Patna airport proof of BJP's corruption, alleges Tejashwi
content: Tejashwi Yadav claimed the newly constructed terminal building was "inaugurated in haste" by PM Modi on May 29 last year, ahead of the elections, but within months, tiles in the terminal complex had started coming loose.

title: Watch: India’s economic mettle will be on test at it faces headwinds | The Hindu Editorial
content: Data are now beginning to accumulate, revealing the slowdown in the Indian economy brought on by the crisis in West Asia. The latest is the Index of Eight Core Industries. The data for March, the first month since the war broke out, show that these eight key industrial sectors together contracted by 0.4% over their level in March last year. This was the worst performance in 19 months but was not unexpected.

title: Madras High Court dismisses case against Tamil Nadu Chief Minister’s secretary Umanath
content: An advocate had accused the IAS officer of having violated the model code of conduct by issuing administrative instructions to Collectors and police officers

title: BJP delegation meets ECI, seeks stringent action against Kharge for ‘terrorist’ remark targeting PM
content: Nirmala Sitharaman alleged that the Congress is “completely frustrated” and making such remarks against the Prime Minister amid Assembly polls in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal because of its "deep hatred" towards PM Modi

title: Kerala fireworks accident: Emotional scenes at Thrissur MCH mortuary as families mourn victims
content: Among the six bodies identified, autopsy has been carried out and the bodies have been handed over to the relatives, officials said.

title: Tamil Nadu 2026 elections: Candidates, electors, and more
content: Tamil Nadu’s 234 constituencies will go to polls on April 23 to elect its 17th State Assembly. We explain, using graphics, everything you need to know about the candidates and voter demographics in the State

title: Justice Yashwant Varma row: The future of inquiry amid his resignation
content: Justice Yashwant Varma’s resignation raises questions over the fate of the statutory inquiry against him. The Constitution asks Parliament to enact a law regulating the investigation of allegations against a judge and an address for seeking removal. The key issue is whether the inquiry’s survival can hinge on the judge’s continued tenure

title: New railway line proposed to connect ‘controversial’ bauxite hills in Odisha
content: Activists lament that even as tribal communities rise in protest against mining projects warning of severe impacts on their lives, the government, has announced a new railway project for mining

title: Three dead, one injured as car hits signboard near Veeravalli toll plaza in East Godavari
content: The four were returning to Gajjaram village after taking a relative, who is physically disabled, to Chennai for medical treatment; overspeeding is the suspected cause.

title: Chettinad Heritage and Cultural Festival : A reason to celebrate Tamil history, culture, and cuisine
content: Learn the stories of formidable Chettiar women, textile trade ties between India and South East Asia, and tuck into the softest kola urundais at The Chettinad Heritage and Cultural Festival between September 18 and 21

title: Large scale disruption in bus services as Telangana RTC employees launch indefinite strike
content: Transport Minister Ponnam Prabhakar said Telangana government has not objected to 29 of the 32 demands raised by TGSRTC workers’ union, leaving only two issues unresolved

title: IPL betting racket busted in Nellore; 12 held, ₹37 lakh transactions traced
content: The main accused, Sk. Fariaz, acted as the administrator; police seized ₹2.05 lakh cash, 13 mobile phones and a television, and froze three bank accounts

title: Karnataka’s tourist footfall sees fourfold rise, crosses 30 crore visitors post pandemic
content: The State has consistently ranked among the top three in the country for domestic tourist arrivals, says Patil

title: Dhruva Natchathiram release: Madras High Court to pass orders on April 24
content: Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy wound up hearing oral arguments in the case and decided to dictate orders on April 24, 2026

title: India will never bow before forces spreading hatred, fear: Rahul on Pahalgam attack anniversary
content: On the first anniversary of the dastardly attack, Rahul Gandhi paid his heartfelt tributes to all the brave hearts who were killed

title: BMTC to add independent external monitoring layer to smart ticketing system as 11,000 ETMs rolled out
content: In March, the BMTC began rolling out a fully integrated digital ticketing system as part of a major push to modernise its operations

title: 2006 Malegaon blast case: Bombay HC discharges four accused, quashes charges against them
content: A Bench of Chief Justice Shree Chandrashekhar and Justice Shyam Chandak allowed the appeals filed by the four accused - Rajendra Chaudhary, Dhan Singh, Manohar Ram Singh Narwaria and Lokesh Sharma — against the special court order.

title: Third conviction in Shivamogga ISIS conspiracy case; accused gets 6 years RI
content: The NIA special court had earlier, in November 2025, sentenced two other accused to six years rigorous imprisonment in the same case.

title: After Pahalgam massacre, security forces took the terror fight back to Kashmir’s jungles
content: The terror attack also led to a review of the security grid in the Union Territory. Young officers were inducted into the Special Operations Group (SOG) of the J&K Police and they trained alongside the Greyhounds and the Para (Special Forces)

title: Kerala HC stays ‘dry day’ order near Tamil Nadu border ahead of Assembly elections
content: Court stays Kerala government order on the grounds that the State did not have the power to issue such a direction, a lawyer associated with the case said

title: Andhra Pradesh will lose five Lok Sabha seats if 131st Amendment is not revived, says Purandeswari
content: Rajamahendravaram BJP MP seeks an apology from Opposition for defeating the Bill; she says the amendment’s delimitation formula, with a larger Lok Sabha, would have protected southern States

title: Earth Day 2026: A few initiatives from Thiruvananthapuram doing their bit for the environment
content: Ahead of Earth Day, which falls on April 22, know a few people who have made a difference through community engagement and sustainability-focussed initiatives

title: U.S. woman sexually assaulted at Kodagu homestay; two arrested
content: The owner and staff of the homestay, where the woman stayed, have been arrested; homestay owner has been taken into custody for allegedly withholding assistance and attempting to cover up the crime

title: Kedarnath Dham portals open for devotees amid chants; Uttarakhand Chief Minister offers prayers
content: Thousands of pilgrims gathered at the Himalayan shrine, decorated with 15 quintals of flowers, according to officials

title: Minorities have been underrepresented in Bengal Assembly
content: Despite its reputation as a progressive State, women and Muslims have sparsely held power in West Bengal

title: How community-led environmental initiatives are making a change in Ernakulam
content: Ahead of Earth Day on April 22, we look at collectives in Ernakulam that are driving change through sustained, grassroots action

title: Andhra Pradesh Ministers meet Singapore Home Minister Shanmugam on Day 2 of governance tour
content: Singapore Home Minister K. Shanmugam briefed the delegation on the city-state’s legal framework and policing; training sessions at NUS Cities and a visit to the Singapore City Gallery are lined up for the day.

title: Kerala fireworks unit blast: Suresh Gopi visits site in Thrissur; Central team likely to probe incident
content: Forensic experts examining the blast site. Union Minister says more clarity is expected after detailed analysis

title: Get out, you're causing traffic jam: Woman snaps at Minister during BJP's Mumbai rally
content: The incident occurred on April 21, when Mahajan was addressing reporters at the rally, organised to target Opposition parties over the defeat of the Constitution Amendment Bill, which aimed to fast-track the implementation of 33% reservation for women in legislative bodies, in the Lok Sabha

title: India will never bow to any form of terror: PM Modi on Pahalgam attack anniversary
content: The Pakistan-based terror group Lashkar-e-Taiba carried out an attack at Pahalgam in Jammu and Kashmir on this day last year, killing 26 innocent people, mostly tourists from across India

title: Visakhapatnam teachers stage protest demanding Pay Revision Commission, interim relief
content: The Progressive Recognised Teachers’ Union says problems raised with the State government two years ago remain unresolved; it seeks a 30% interim relief and an exemption from the Teachers Eligibility Test for in-service teachers

title: Fantasy baseball news: Raisel Iglesias joins growing list of injured closers
content: fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around MLB . the full 15-game slate consists entirely of evening affairs, beginning at 6:10 p.m. ET with the Cleveland Guardians hosting the Houston Astros .

title: NBA draft top 100: Where top prospects land on the big board after March
content: yaxel Lendeborg wins a national championship with Michigan . AJ Dybantsa takes that mantle as the dust has settled on the season . the top of the draft remains open for discussion, with a group of talented players still jockeying for position in the lottery .
